Severe Weather Batters Tripura Under Red Alert, Demand Grows for Closure of Schools and Colleges
By Our Correspondent
Agartala, April 29, 2026
Tripura is witnessing a rapidly deteriorating weather situation as continuous heavy to very heavy rainfall, accompanied by storms and strong winds, disrupts normal life across the state. Despite the alarming conditions and an official Red Alert issued by the India Meteorological Department, questions are being raised over why the state government has not yet declared a holiday for schools, colleges, and other educational institutions.
According to the Meteorological Centre in Agartala, the Red Alert will remain in force from April 29 to May 3, 2026, warning of intense rainfall across all districts. The administration has cautioned that the situation may worsen over the next three days, with the risk of waterlogging, localized flooding, and disruption of essential services.
In West Tripura district, the office of the District Magistrate has issued an urgent advisory urging residents to remain indoors as much as possible, avoid travel near rivers and low-lying areas, and ensure the safety of children and the elderly. Emergency helplines have been activated round the clock to respond to any crisis.
The impact of the adverse weather is already visible. Vehicular movement on roads has reduced significantly, with many private transport services nearly suspended. Train services are also not operating normally, further compounding travel difficulties. In several areas, heavy rain and gusty winds have affected daily life, leading to a near standstill in public movement.
Amid this situation, a strong public demand has emerged for the closure of all educational institutions for at least the next three days. Guardians, students, and civil society members argue that continuing regular classes under such hazardous conditions poses serious risks to safety. With transportation disrupted and weather conditions worsening, many believe that an immediate decision on school and college is necessary.
Observers note that in similar circumstances in the past, precautionary closures were announced to prevent accidents and ensure student safety. However, as of now, no such directive has been issued by the state authorities, leading to growing concern and criticism.
With the Red Alert still in place and the weather forecast indicating continued instability, all eyes are now on the Tripura government for a timely decision that prioritizes public safety, particularly that of students.
